title: Global AI Regulation & Legislation
description: From the EU AI Act to US executive orders and China's Generative AI regulations — tracking legislative progress across major economies and the real-world impact on the industry.

timeline.title: EU AI Act Final Text Published in Official Journal
timeline.summary: The final trilogue text confirms a tiered regulatory framework for high-risk AI systems. General-purpose AI models (GPAI) must meet transparency and safety assessment obligations.

timeline.title: US Releases Updated AI Executive Order — Federal Agencies Must Complete AI Audits Within 180 Days
timeline.summary: The updated order adds dedicated provisions for agent-type systems to the 2023 baseline, requiring any autonomous decision-making AI to maintain complete decision logs.

timeline.title: China's CAC Releases Draft Revision of Generative AI Service Management Measures
timeline.summary: The revision brings AI agents into regulatory scope for the first time, requiring companies providing agent services to register and submit to algorithm audits.

timeline.title: EU AI Act Enforcement Confirmed for Q3 2026 — Compliance Window Now ~90 Days
timeline.summary: The European Commission confirmed the enforcement date. Companies must complete high-risk system registration and compliance self-assessment before Q3. Several US AI companies said they may delay agent product launches in the European market.

timeline.title: CAISI Signs Pre-Release Model Evaluation Agreements With Google DeepMind, Microsoft, and xAI
timeline.summary: The Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI), the Trump administration's reorganized successor to NIST's AI Safety Institute, formalized agreements that grant the US government access to evaluate frontier models before public release. The deals extend a framework already in place with Anthropic and OpenAI, putting all five of the largest US labs under a common pre-release review regime.

timeline.title: Kevin Warsh Senate Floor Vote Expected This Week — Warsh to Inherit Inflation Fight Before Powell Exits May 15
timeline.summary: Following the 13–11 party-line Banking Committee approval (April 29), the full Senate is expected to vote on Kevin Warsh's confirmation as Fed chair this week. Warsh would take over from Powell on May 15 amid rates held at 3.5–3.75%, persistent Middle East-linked inflation, and the most FOMC dissents (4) since 1992. Washington Post analysis noted Warsh 'inherits a Fed under pressure' and faces a divided board. Powell will remain as a governor until 2028.

timeline.title: Vatican Forms Dedicated AI Study Group Ahead of Pope Leo XIV's First Encyclical 'Magnifica Humanitas' — Today Is the 60th World Day of Social Communications Under Leo's Theme 'Preserving Human Voices and Faces'
timeline.summary: Vatican announcements over the weekend confirm Pope Leo XIV has created a dedicated AI study group as the Holy See prepares to release his first encyclical, reportedly titled Magnifica Humanitas ('Magnificent Humanity'). The document is being explicitly dated to the 135th anniversary of Pope Leo XIII's 1891 Rerum Novarum, framing AI as the defining moral and labour challenge of a new industrial revolution rather than a tech-policy file. Today is the 60th World Day of Social Communications; Leo's message released for today is titled 'Preserving Human Voices and Faces' and extends the encyclical's concern to AI-generated media. The encyclical is expected to place AI inside Catholic social teaching — labour, justice, peace — building on Leo's May 14 La Sapienza warning that AI and high-tech weaponry are 'leading the world into a spiral of annihilation.' Together with the Fed transition (Powell's term ended May 15; Warsh confirmed 54–45 Wednesday, awaiting final paperwork to be sworn in ahead of the June 16–17 FOMC), the week shows two parallel governance frames — secular and ecclesial — moving on the AI question at the same time.

timeline.title: Magnifica Humanitas Slips from May 15 to ~May 25; Pope Leo XIV Pairs the AI Encyclical with Laudato Si' Week at Sunday's Regina Caeli — Continuous Catholic Social-Teaching Arc from Industrial Age to AI Age
timeline.summary: Infovaticana confirmed on May 15 that Leo XIV's first encyclical 'Magnifica Humanitas' — originally targeted for signature on the 135th anniversary of Rerum Novarum (May 15, 1891) — has slipped to roughly May 25; multiple Vatican outlets converge on the late-May publication window. From the Apostolic Palace window on Sunday May 17 the Pope used the Regina Caeli to bind two threads of his early pontificate together, telling pilgrims that 'in this era of artificial intelligence, I encourage everyone to commit themselves to promoting forms of communication that always respect the truth of the human person, on which every technological innovation should be focused,' and asking members of the Laudato Si' Movement to renew their commitment as Laudato Si' Week (May 17–24) begins. The pairing is doctrinally deliberate: just as Rerum Novarum was the Church's labour answer to the steam age and Laudato Si' was Francis's ecology answer to the climate age, Magnifica Humanitas is being positioned as Leo XIV's human-dignity answer to the AI age. Parallel secular frame: Warsh begins his first full Monday as Fed chair, with markets pricing <3% odds of any 2026 rate cut.
